refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to ensure safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, clarifying the performance requirement for teleoperation communication.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication link under defined network conditions using telemetry data during operational scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: The system shall minimize communication latency between the teleoperator and vehicle control systems.
FR2: The system shall ensure reliable data transmission over the network link to support latency requirements.
FR3: The system shall monitor and report latency metrics to enable safety management.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized communication protocols and data compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge processing at vehicle and teleoperation modules to minimize data round-trip time.
DP3: Employ network quality of service (QoS) mechanisms to prioritize teleoperation traffic.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized communication protocols and data compression to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between teleoperation control logic and network communication stack.
Dependency on network link quality and external network conditions.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol optimization, which logically maps to teleoperation and network interfaces with manageable coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unpredictable network behavior.
Assumes availability of telemetry data for latency monitoring.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge processing (DP2) in future iterations to further reduce latency and improve robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes communication protocol optimization and latency reduction logic for teleoperation control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for sending and receiving latency metrics and control data.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface for teleoperation data transmission.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on communication protocol specifics.
Contains vehicle control but not primary for communication protocol optimization.
Highest containment score; primary owner of teleoperation communication logic.
Supports safety alerts but not involved in latency optimization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces poorly with Vehicle module.
Risk of interface mismatches on Network Link affecting latency guarantees.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface contracts between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to ensure latency requirements are met end-to-end.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement; selected DP logically addresses latency reduction with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Interface dependencies on Network Link and Telemetry are well-defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module is the best fit for DP containment, supporting clear ownership and architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry data and network monitoring tools at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functionality to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance monitoring capabilities in Teleoperation module for real-time latency reporting.
Plan integration tests to validate latency under network conditions.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently captures the latency requirement with a clear DP and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are well-defined, and risks are manageable.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: The system shall minimize communication latency between the teleoperator and vehicle control systems.
FR2: The system shall ensure reliable data transmission over the network link to support latency requirements.
FR3: The system shall monitor and report latency metrics to enable safety management.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level or implementation.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data is available and accurate for latency measurement.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to unpredictable network behavior.
Interface mismatches between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational network conditions.
